LAW AND MOTION 302, PETITIONER RUDOLF ISCH'S PETITION TO CONFIRM ARBITRATION AWARD IS GRANTED. (CCP SEC. 1285.) PER CCP SEC. 1288, A MOTION TO VACATE OR CORRECT AN AWARD MUST BE FILED WITHIN 100 DAYS AFTER SERVICE OF THE AWARD. (SEE ALSO ABERS V. ROHRS (2013) 217 CAL.APP.4TH 1199, 1211, HOLDING THAT THE 100 DAY PERIOD IS JURISDICTIONAL AND THE COURT HAS NO DISCRETION TO EXTEND THE MANDATORY DEADLINE.) THE SAME 100-DAY LIMITATION APPLIES WHEN VACATION OR CORRECTION OF THE AWARD IS SOUGHT BY RESPONSE. (CCP SEC. 1288.2; DE MELLO V. SOUZA (1973) 36 CAL.APP.3D 79, 83.) IT IS UNDISPUTED THAT THE AMENDED ARBITRATION AWARD WAS SERVED ON FEBRUARY 10, 2014. (PETITION TO CONFIRM ARBITRATION AWARD, PAR. 9(B); PETITION TO VACATE ARBITRATION AWARD, PAR. 9(B).) RESPONDENT'S ANSWER AND PETITION TO VACATE ARBITRATION AWARD WERE NOT FILED UNTIL JULY 16, 2014, BEYOND THE 100 DAY PERIOD, AND ARE THEREFORE TIME-BARRED. ORDER AND JUDGMENT SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: ERNEST J. GOLDSMITH; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER, CSR # 5976 (302/EHG)

calendar.matters:
Motion To Set Aside Judgment Obtained By Petitioner Rudolf V. Isch
Off Calendar Jan-02-2015 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES: Atty. ROBERT L. FERRIS, Tel No. (650) 488-0431 appearing for the Petitioner;
Atty. DAVID NIED of Ad Astra Law Group, LLP, Tel No. (415) 795-3579 appearing for the Respondent.

RULING - A R G U E D - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. Respondent Vincent A. Isch's Motion To Set Aside Judgment Obtained By Petitioner Rudolf V. Isch is denied. First, Respondent has presented no factual basis to conclude that the judgment or the order he seeks to set aside are void. Second, Respondent has failed to articulate a satisfactory excuse for failing to present a defense. Respondent failed to appear at the arbitration hearing on time, and asserts that on this basis he was given false information and denied an opportunity to participate in the hearing. However, relief from extrinsic mistake is denied where the complaining party has contributed to the mistake giving rise to the judgment thus obtained. (Hammell v. Britton (1941) 19 Cal.2d 72, 80.) Further, Respondent's contention that his attorney became incapacitated and unable to timely file his petition is not supported by Vetesi's Declaration or any other evidence presented, and is undermined by the very arguments made by Vetesi during the hearing on the Petition to Confirm the Arbitration Award. Finally, observations made by and conclusions drawn by Respondent's attorneys as to his mental state are insufficient to demonstrate that Respondent suffered from a mental disability that prevented him from presenting his case. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Sheryl L. Sawyer, CSR #5976 (302/EHG)

Notice Of Hearing On Petition To Vacate Amended Arbitration Award Dated February 9, 2014
Off Calendar Aug-21-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES - ATTY. ROBERT L. FERRIS, TEL. NO. (650) 550-4387 APPEARING FOR THE PETITIONER;
ATTY. OLIVIA VETESI OF THE VETESI LAW, P.C., TEL. NO. 735-6282 APPEARING FOR THE RESPONDENT.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. RESPONDENT VINCENT ISCH's Petition To Vacate Amended Arbitration Award Dated February 9, 2014 is Denied as moot in light of the ruling on the Petition to Confirm Arbitration Award. Judge: Ernest J. Goldsmith; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR # 5976 =302/eg

Ntc Of Hrg On Petition To Confirm Arb Award
Off Calendar Aug-21-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. ROBERT L. FERRIS, TEL. NO. (650) 550-4387 APPEARING FOR THE PETITIONER;
ATTY. OLIVIA VETESI OF THE VETESI LAW, P.C., TEL. NO. 735-6282 APPEARING FOR THE RESPONDENT.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. PETITIONER RUDOLF ISCH's Petition To Confirm Arbitration Award is Granted. (CCP sec. 1285.) Per CCP sec. 1288, a motion to vacate or correct an award must be filed within 100 days after service of the award. (See also Abers v. Rohrs (2013) 217 Cal.App.4th 1199, 1211, holding that the 100 day period is jurisdictional and the court has no discretion to extend the mandatory deadline.) The same 100-day limitation applies when vacation or correction of the award is sought by response. (CCP sec. 1288.2; De Mello v. Souza (1973) 36 Cal.App.3d 79, 83.) It is undisputed that the Amended Arbitration Award was served on February 10, 2014. (Petition to Confirm Arbitration Award, par. 9(b); Petition to Vacate Arbitration Award, par. 9(b).) Respondent's Answer and Petition to Vacate Arbitration Award were not filed until July 16, 2014, beyond the 100 day period, and are therefore time-barred. ORDER AND JUDGMENT SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Ernest J. Goldsmith; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR # 5976 =302/eg
